title: Tesla Model Y Charging Cost
h1: How much does it cost to charge a Tesla Model Y?
description: Estimate the real-world cost to charge a Tesla Model Y at home or at a Supercharger.
directAnswer: A Tesla Model Y Long Range (~75 kWh) charged from 20% to 80% at the U.S. average home rate of $0.16/kWh costs about $7.20.

title: Tesla Model S Charging Cost
h1: How much does it cost to charge a Tesla Model S?
description: Estimate the cost of a full or partial Tesla Model S charge at home or on a Supercharger.
directAnswer: A Tesla Model S (100 kWh) charged from 20% to 80% at $0.16/kWh costs about $9.60. A 10–80% Supercharger session at $0.36/kWh runs about $25.20.

title: Tesla Model X Charging Cost
h1: How much does it cost to charge a Tesla Model X?
description: Calculate Tesla Model X home and Supercharger charging cost based on your battery and rate.
directAnswer: Charging a Tesla Model X (100 kWh) from 20% to 80% at $0.16/kWh costs about $9.60.

title: Ford Mustang Mach-E Charging Cost
h1: How much does it cost to charge a Ford Mustang Mach-E?
description: Estimate Mustang Mach-E charging cost at home (Level 2) or at a public DC fast charger.
directAnswer: A Mustang Mach-E (~88 kWh) charged from 20% to 80% at $0.16/kWh costs about $8.45.

title: Chevy Bolt Charging Cost
h1: How much does it cost to charge a Chevy Bolt?
description: Estimate Chevy Bolt EV / EUV charging cost at home or on a public Level 2 charger.
directAnswer: A Chevy Bolt (65 kWh) charged from 20% to 80% at $0.16/kWh costs about $6.24.

title: Nissan Leaf Charging Cost
h1: How much does it cost to charge a Nissan Leaf?
description: Estimate Nissan Leaf and Leaf Plus charging cost at home or on a CHAdeMO fast charger.
directAnswer: A Nissan Leaf Plus (62 kWh) charged from 20% to 80% at $0.16/kWh costs about $5.95.

title: Rivian R1T Charging Cost
h1: How much does it cost to charge a Rivian R1T?
description: Estimate Rivian R1T charging cost at home or on a DC fast charger.
directAnswer: A Rivian R1T Large Pack (~135 kWh) charged from 20% to 80% at $0.16/kWh costs about $12.96.

title: Hyundai Ioniq 5 Charging Cost
h1: How much does it cost to charge a Hyundai Ioniq 5?
description: Estimate Hyundai Ioniq 5 charging cost at home or on an 800V DC fast charger.
directAnswer: A Hyundai Ioniq 5 (77.4 kWh) charged from 20% to 80% at $0.16/kWh costs about $7.43.

title: Home EV Charging Cost in California
h1: Home EV charging cost in California
description: Estimate California home EV charging cost using PG&E, SCE, and SDG&E EV-friendly rates.
directAnswer: California EV-rate plans typically run $0.30/kWh on-peak and $0.16/kWh off-peak. A 60 kWh charge off-peak costs about $9.60.
